faqs.question: What is SimplePractice used for?

faqs.question: Does SimplePractice offer a free trial?

faqs.answer: Plans are billed monthly per clinician, starting around $49/month for the Starter plan, with Essential and Plus tiers priced higher for additional features.
faqs.question: How much does SimplePractice cost?

faqs.answer: No, while therapists and counselors are a large part of its user base, SimplePractice is also used by dietitians, speech-language pathologists, occupational and physical therapists, and other wellness practitioners.

faqs.answer: Yes, SimplePractice includes built-in HIPAA-compliant video sessions so clinicians can hold remote appointments without a separate video platform.

Large marketplace of third-party integrations

Cloud-only, so firms needing on-premises hosting or strict data residency options must look elsewhere

Per-user pricing can become expensive for larger firms compared to flat-fee alternatives

faqs.answer: No. Clio does not offer a permanent free plan; it offers a time-limited free trial, after which firms must choose a paid plan.
faqs.question: Is Clio free to use?

faqs.answer: Yes. Clio Manage includes trust accounting tools designed to help firms track client retainer funds in line with IOLTA and similar rules.
faqs.question: Does Clio support trust accounting?

faqs.answer: Yes. Clio's entry-level plan is aimed at solo practitioners and small firms that need case management, billing, and time tracking in one system.

faqs.answer: Clio Manage handles case management, billing, and trust accounting after a matter is opened, while Clio Grow handles client intake and CRM before and during the intake process.
faqs.question: What is the difference between Clio Manage and Clio Grow?

advancedmd.name: AdvancedMD
advancedmd.short_description: Cloud-based practice management, EHR, and medical billing software for independent healthcare practices and billing companies.
advancedmd.starting_price: Custom pricing, typically quoted per provider per month; publicly cited starting points run from roughly $429/provider/month (practice management + billing) up to $729/provider/month (practice management + EHR)

features.description: Appointment scheduling, patient registration, and insurance eligibility verification in one workflow.
features.title: Practice Management & Scheduling

features.description: Clinical charting with specialty-specific templates, structured notes, and e-prescribing.
features.title: Certified Electronic Health Records (EHR)

features.description: Claims scrubbing, clearinghouse submission, denial management, and collections tracking.
features.title: Medical Billing & Revenue Cycle Management

features.description: Self-service patient portal for forms, messaging, appointment requests, and online check-in.
features.title: Patient Portal & Online Intake

features.description: Native video visit capability that documents and bills virtual encounters inside the same patient chart.
features.title: Telemedicine / Video Visits

features.description: Electronic prescription routing integrated directly into the clinical charting workflow.
features.title: E-Prescribing

features.description: Built-in dashboards for tracking collections, denial rates, and provider productivity.
features.title: Reporting & Business Intelligence

features.description: iOS and Android apps for charting on the go and monitoring billing/claims status remotely.
features.title: Mobile Apps for Providers & Staff

features.description: A single account structure that lets billing services manage claims and collections across many separate client practices.
features.title: Multi-Client Billing Company Support

features.description: Marketplace and API connections to labs, clearinghouses, and other health-IT systems.
features.title: APIs & Third-Party Integrations

pros: All-in-one PM, EHR, and billing suite reduces the need for separate integrated vendors
Purpose-built support for medical billing companies managing multiple client practices from one account
Modular pricing lets practices start with PM and billing, then add EHR later
Long operating history with established payer and clearinghouse connections
Native telehealth and patient engagement tools built into the same patient chart
cons: Interface feels denser and more dated than some newer, design-forward EHR competitors
Meaningful learning curve — new staff typically need real training time to get productive
Costs can add up quickly once billing services, telehealth, and engagement add-ons are layered on
No published flat self-serve pricing — every quote requires a sales conversation
Customer support experiences are mixed in independent reviews, with some users citing slow resolution times

faqs.answer: AdvancedMD uses custom, quote-based pricing charged per provider per month, which varies depending on which modules (practice management, EHR, billing, telehealth) a practice selects. Publicly cited starting points run from roughly $429/provider/month for practice management plus billing up to around $729/provider/month for practice management plus EHR; some billing services are instead priced per claim or as a percentage of collections.
faqs.question: How much does AdvancedMD cost?

faqs.answer: Both. AdvancedMD is a modular suite — practices can license practice management and medical billing on their own, or add the certified EHR and telehealth modules, all running on one shared patient and financial database.
faqs.question: Is AdvancedMD an EHR or a practice management system?

faqs.answer: Yes, AdvancedMD includes native video visit functionality that lets providers document and bill telehealth encounters inside the same patient chart used for in-person visits.
faqs.question: Does AdvancedMD support telehealth?

faqs.answer: AdvancedMD has changed hands several times: Francisco Partners acquired it in 2008, sold it to ADP in 2011, Global Payments acquired it in 2018, and Francisco Partners took ownership again in a 2024 deal.
faqs.question: Who owns AdvancedMD?

faqs.answer: Yes — AdvancedMD is specifically built to let a single medical billing or RCM company manage claims, statements, and collections across many separate client practices from one account, which is one of its more distinctive strengths compared to single-practice-only EHR platforms.
faqs.question: Is AdvancedMD a good fit for medical billing companies?

pricing_plans.price: Custom quote; publicly cited starting around $429/provider/month
Custom quote; publicly cited starting around $729/provider/month

features: All practice management, EHR, and billing modules
pricing_plans.price: Custom quote; typically the highest tier

features: Often paired with practice management or full-suite modules
pricing_plans.price: Publicly cited range of roughly $0.87–$2.17 per claim

Professional edition is described by PortSwigger as a leading web penetration testing toolkit
Enterprise edition supports automated, CI-integrated scanning at scale
Backed by PortSwigger's free Web Security Academy training content
Long-established tool widely used across penetration testing and bug bounty communities
cons: Full scanning capabilities require the paid Professional or Enterprise editions
Pricing is not published on the main product page and requires further inquiry
Community Edition lacks the automated scanner found in paid tiers

faqs.answer: The Community Edition is free with manual testing tools; the automated scanner requires the paid Professional or Enterprise editions.
faqs.question: Is Burp Suite free?

faqs.answer: Burp Suite Professional is PortSwigger's paid edition aimed at hands-on security professionals and penetration testers, including an automated web vulnerability scanner.
faqs.question: What is Burp Suite Professional?

faqs.answer: It is PortSwigger's enterprise-focused edition for automated scanning at scale, aimed at AppSec and vulnerability management teams.
faqs.question: What is Burp Suite Enterprise (DAST)?

faqs.answer: Burp Suite is made by PortSwigger.
faqs.question: Who makes Burp Suite?

faqs.answer: Yes, PortSwigger offers free training through its Web Security Academy.
faqs.question: Does PortSwigger offer security training?

doctolib.name: Doctolib
doctolib.short_description: Doctolib is an online platform for booking appointments with doctors and healthcare providers and offers video consultations.

features.description: Patients can search for healthcare providers and book available appointment times directly online.
features.title: Online appointment booking

features.description: Telemedicine video consultations are available directly through the platform.
features.title: Video consultations

features.description: Listings cover 30+ medical specialties, including general practitioners, dentists, pediatricians, gynecologists, dermatologists, and cardiologists.
features.title: Specialist directory

features.description: Dedicated iOS and Android apps let patients book and manage appointments on the go.
features.title: Mobile apps

features.description: The site provides help center resources and articles on health topics for patients.
features.title: Health content and help center

pros: Covers 30+ medical specialties in a single directory
Available as a website plus dedicated iOS and Android apps
Offers video consultations alongside in-person appointment booking
B Corp certified
Operates across multiple European countries (France, Germany, Italy)
cons: Primarily serves French, German, and Italian markets, with limited availability elsewhere
Pricing for healthcare practitioners is not published on the site
No integrations were disclosed in the publicly available content

faqs.answer: Doctolib lets patients search for and book appointments online with doctors and other healthcare providers, and it also offers video consultations.
faqs.question: What is Doctolib used for?

faqs.answer: Yes. In addition to France, Doctolib operates in Germany and Italy.
faqs.question: Is Doctolib available outside of France?

faqs.answer: Yes, Doctolib offers apps for both iOS and Android.
faqs.question: Does Doctolib have a mobile app?

faqs.answer: Doctolib is B Corp certified.
faqs.question: Is Doctolib a certified company?

corteza-crm.name: Corteza CRM
corteza-crm.short_description: Corteza is an open-source, low-code platform (Apache 2.0) with a built-in CRM module for records-based business apps.

features.description: Gives a 360-degree overview of leads and accounts, with a module for automating sales and service processes.
features.title: Low-code CRM

features.description: A Google Workspace-style collaboration layer bundled into the platform.
features.title: Unified Workspace

features.description: Built-in Slack-style internal messaging for teams.
features.title: Enterprise Messaging

features.description: Role-based access control that lets organizations apply complex internal security policies.
features.title: Flattened RBAC

features.description: Build and iterate custom records-based business apps beyond CRM.
features.title: Low-code app builder

features.description: The platform is built to conform to WCAG 2.1 accessibility guidelines.
features.title: Accessibility

pros: Fully open source under the Apache 2.0 license with no vendor lock-in
Code is contributed to the Commons Conservancy Software Foundation, so access cannot be revoked by a single vendor
Bundles CRM, internal messaging, and workspace tools in one self-hosted platform
Actively maintained on GitHub with recent commits and 2,000+ stars
cons: Requires self-hosting (e.g. via Docker) and technical setup to run
No first-party managed-cloud pricing page on cortezaproject.org; hosted instances are offered by third parties such as Planet Crust
Smaller community and ecosystem than major commercial CRMs like Salesforce

faqs.answer: Yes. Corteza is fully open source under the Apache 2.0 license and free to download and self-host.
faqs.question: Is Corteza free?

faqs.answer: Corteza is released under the Apache License 2.0.
faqs.question: What license does Corteza use?

faqs.answer: Corteza is designed to be self-hosted via Docker; hosted cloud instances are also offered by third parties such as Planet Crust, separate from the core open-source project.
faqs.question: Can I run Corteza without managing my own server?

faqs.answer: No. Corteza is a broader low-code platform that includes a CRM module alongside a unified workspace and enterprise messaging.
faqs.question: Is Corteza only a CRM?
